title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
summary: Gemma 4's 31B model achieves fourth place on the LM Arena open model leaderboard despite being 2 to 20 times smaller than competing models at comparable quality. The model runs on a single GPU and can be deployed on mobile devices, multi-GPU hospital systems, and Mac machines via LM Studio.
writeup: Gus Martins and Ian Ballantyne of Google DeepMind present Gemma 4's 31B open model and its case for ownership through open weights. The model reaches fourth place on the LM Arena open model leaderboard while being 2 to 20 times smaller than competitors at comparable quality, a point that ties directly to token efficiency. Deployment spans a single GPU, mobile devices, multi-GPU hospital systems, and Mac machines through LM Studio, showing the range of on-device and self-hosted options for a foundation model of this size.
